stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The growth rate of mangrove forests depends on tidal flooding patterns.
  • In the rainy season, mangrove forests along Belize’s coast capture and store atmospheric carbon at a rapid pace.
  • The growing mangroves contribute to coastal stabilization each rainy season.
  • Adjacent seagrass meadows also capture and store atmospheric carbon offshore.
  • In 2019, marine biologist Diego Ramirez found that seagrass growth depends not on tidal flooding but on consistent water clarity.
  • Because water clarity is consistent, growing seagrass meadows contribute to coastal stabilization all year.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between mangroves and seagrass meadows along Belize’s coast. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Belize’s mangroves and seagrass meadows both grow during the year, capturing atmospheric carbon and contributing to coastal stabilization.
Because seagrass meadows grow offshore from mangrove forests, their growth rate depends on water clarity.
Marine biologist Diego Ramirez found that the growth rate of Belize’s seagrass meadows, unlike that of mangroves, does not depend on tidal flooding patterns.
Mangroves along Belize’s coast grow during the rainy months into the surrounding water, the clarity of which remains consistent throughout the year.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between mangroves and seagrass meadows along Belize’s coast, noting that both grow and thereby capture atmospheric carbon and contribute to coastal stabilization.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence provides information about seagrass growth; it doesn’t emphasize a similarity between mangroves and seagrass. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference between mangroves and seagrass; it doesn’t emphasize a similarity. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence explains conditions under which mangroves grow; it doesn’t emphasize a similarity between mangroves and seagrass meadows.
